git push Error GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.
I recently tweaked a GitHub setting and started getting
GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.
when pushing code. Here's why it happens and how to fix it.
I was adjusting GitHub's Email settings and accidentally enabled
We'll remove your public profile email and use

when performing web-based Git operations (e.g. edits and merges) and sending email on your behalf. If you want command line Git operations to use your private email you must set your email in Git.
Commits pushed to GitHub using this email will still be associated with your account.
is on, if you push code to a public repo or contribute to an organization via the web, other people won't see your real email — they'll see GitHub's noreply alias.
But after enabling this, my push started failing:

git add
git commit -m 'first'
git push origin main

remote: Resolving deltas: 100% (2/2), completed with 2 local objects.
remote: error: GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.
remote: You can make your email public or disable this protection by visiting:
remote: http://github.com/settings/emails
To https://github.com/xxxxxxx
 ! [remote rejected] main -> main (push declined due to email privacy restrictions)
error: failed to push some refs to 'https://github.com/xxxxxx'
remote: Resolving deltas: 100% (2/2), completed with 2 local objects.
remote: error: GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.
remote: You can make your email public or disable this protection by visiting:
! [remote rejected] main -> main (push declined due to email privacy restrictions)
error: failed to push some refs to 'https://github.com/xxxxxx'
Let me note down how to fix it.
Normally your global Git config has your primary GitHub email set. To check:
git config --global --list # You should see a user.email entry
and find your GitHub-provided noreply email.
Copy it, then run in your terminal:
again to confirm it switched to the noreply email.
One more important step — update the commit author for your existing commit, otherwise you can still hit push errors:
That said — for commits you'd already made before the change, the original email can't be hidden anymore. After switching, any new clone I did would still show my noreply email going forward.
